explanation: Memory you malloc'd is gone: no pointer anywhere in your program still points to it, and you never freed it. Classic leak: find the malloc with no matching free.
Your program wrote to memory it doesn't own, often one element past the end of an array (an off-by-one), or through a pointer that's already been freed.
Your program read from memory it doesn't own, often reading past the end of an allocation, or reading through a dangling pointer after free().
You called free() on a pointer that wasn't the result of a matching malloc: maybe it was already freed (a double free), or it points into the middle of a block instead of the start.

match: conditional jump or move depends on uninitialised value
explanation: Your program branched on a variable that was never given a value. Track down where it should have been initialized.
